description: Cross-target conformance test vectors for the Tonnetz engine (see docs/ai/adr/archive/001-tonnetz-engine-interface.md and docs/ai/adr/005-rhythmic-feel.md). All target implementations (m4l, vst, app) must pass these. Binding assertion: pitch-class set of output equals expected_pcs (order-independent). Realized octaves may vary by integer multiples of 12 due to target-specific boundary behavior in buildTriad; pitch-class identity is the contract.

conventions.triad: [a, b, c] MIDI note numbers. For inputs in close position, a=root, b=root+3 or +4, c=root+7. identify_triad cases also include inversions.
conventions.pitch_classes: integer 0..11 (C=0, ..., B=11), sorted ascending for set equality
conventions.transforms: P | L | R. Sequences of single-char strings.
conventions.cells: Each cell is a record { op, velocity, gate, probability, timing }. Loader fills defaults for missing fields: velocity=1.0, gate=0.9, probability=1.0, timing=0.0 (ADR 005 §'Default cell values'). 'op' ∈ {P, L, R, hold, rest}. 'hold' leaves the chord cursor untouched but re-emits audio; 'rest' leaves the cursor untouched and is silent. Sequencer iterates cells cyclically; one cell consumed per stepsPerTransform host ticks.
conventions.jitter: float 0..1. Per-step probability that the cell's op is replaced by a uniform-random pick from CELL_OPS = {P, L, R, hold} (rest is excluded so jitter never injects unintended silence — ADR 005). Jitter draws are SKIPPED when jitter == 0 or when the cell's authored op is 'rest'.
conventions.stepDirection: 'forward' | 'reverse' | 'pingpong' | 'random'. Default 'forward' when the field is omitted. Determines the cellIdx sequence consumed at successive transform boundaries. Forward: 0,1,…,N-1,0,…. Reverse: N-1,N-2,…,0,N-1,…. Pingpong: traverse without replaying endpoints — for N=4, sequence is 0,1,2,3,2,1,0,1,… (period 2*(N-1)). Random: each transform boundary picks cellIdx from a uniform [0, N) PRNG draw — independent per step, consecutive same-cell selections allowed. Random consumes 1 PRNG draw at the START of each step's draw sequence (ahead of jitter).
conventions.seed: int. Seeds a mulberry32 PRNG. The PRNG is reseeded fresh from `seed` at the start of every walk(state, pos) call so any-pos restart reproduces. Per-step PRNG draw order (ADR 005 §'PRNG draw order'): (1) stepDirection — 1 draw iff stepDirection=='random'; (2) jitter — 2 draws (substitute? + which) iff jitter>0 AND op!='rest', else 0; (3) probability — 1 draw, always; (4) humanizeVelocity — 1 draw, always; (5) humanizeGate — 1 draw, always; (6) humanizeTiming — 1 draw, always; (7) humanizeProbability — 1 draw, always (Phase 5). Probability rolls trigger silent-advance on P/L/R (chord cursor still applies the transform; no audio). Humanize draws are exposed via StepEvent.humanizeVel/Gate/Timing/Prob (and the raw probability roll rProb) as uniform [0, 1) values — host applies the cell humanize-amount and signed-noise math; engine always consumes the draws so the cross-target stream stays stable.

apply_transform.name: P on first inversion C major
L on second inversion C major

walk_deterministic.name: PLR cycle, jitter=0, spt=1
walk_deterministic.description: Plain cell cycle with no holds and no jitter. cell index advances on every host tick.

walk_deterministic.name: rest cells preserve chord cursor
walk_deterministic.description: ADR 005: 'rest' op leaves the chord cursor unchanged (audio is silent — see walk_step_events). Cell pointer still advances.

walk_deterministic.name: stepsPerTransform=2 holds chord between cells
walk_deterministic.description: Cells consumed only at boundaries (every spt host ticks). Between boundaries, walker holds.

walk_deterministic.name: all-hold cells freeze startChord
walk_deterministic.description: Pathological case: every cell is hold. Walker never moves.

walk_deterministic.name: all-rest cells freeze startChord
walk_deterministic.description: All-rest program: chord cursor never moves, regardless of jitter (rest is excluded from the substitution pool).

samples.note: rest excluded from jitter pool: cursor never moves

walk_deterministic.name: probability=0 still advances chord cursor on P/L/R
walk_deterministic.description: ADR 005: probability fail is silent-advance — chord cursor STILL applies the transform on P/L/R. walk(state, pos) (chord cursor only) is therefore independent of probability for non-rest ops. Verified by setting probability=0 and asserting the same cursor as the deterministic PLR cycle.

samples.note: P: C minor (cursor advances even though step is silent)
L: Ab major (cursor advances even though step is silent)

walk_deterministic.name: single-cell program loops one op
walk_deterministic.description: cells.length=1 case. The same op applies on every transform tick.

walk_step_events.description: Per-step event semantics (ADR 005). walkStepEvent(state, pos) returns the cell consumed at transform boundary `pos`, with: cellIdx, resolvedOp (post-jitter), chord (cursor after this step), played (false on rest or probability fail). Returns null when pos is not a transform boundary or pos <= 0. Cases assert the structural rules — concrete probability/humanize draw values are seeded but not pinned here.

cases.name: rest is always silent regardless of probability
probability=0 never plays but P/L/R cursor still advances
stepDirection=pingpong with cells.length=1 is trivial (always cellIdx=0)

mulberry32.description: PRNG used for jitter. Standard mulberry32 (https://gist.github.com/tommyettinger/46a3c5e1ba2554c7b2f6dab3f54e1eb6). Each implementation MUST produce these exact floats from the given seed for the first N calls. This is the binding contract for cross-target jitter reproducibility.
mulberry32.algorithm: function mulberry32(seed) { let a = seed >>> 0; return () => { a = (a + 0x6D2B79F5) | 0; let t = a; t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1); t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61); return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296; } }
mulberry32.samples: FILLED_BY_ENGINE_IMPLEMENTATION (run scripts/dump-mulberry32.ts after Phase 3 engine lands)

find_triad_in_held_notes.description: Subset search for triad recognition from held MIDI notes per ADR 004 Axis 1. The host pipeline tracks currently-held notes and runs this helper on every note-on/off. Algorithm: iterate every 3-element subset of `notes`, run identifyTriad on each (catching the throw for non-triads); among matching subsets, pick the one whose root MIDI value (the held note matching identifyTriad's rootPc) is lowest; return buildTriad(rootPc, quality, rootMidi) for the winner, or null if no subset matches. Inputs are treated as a set (no duplicate MIDI values); enumeration order is sorted-ascending for deterministic tiebreaking on the rare case where two subsets share the same root MIDI.

cases.name: Csus2: no major/minor triad subset → null
Csus4: no major/minor triad subset → null
Cdim: no major/minor triad subset → null
Caug: no major/minor triad subset → null
first inversion C/E: Cmaj rooted at the held C (72)
octave doubling: lowest root MIDI (48) wins
input order independence: same set, different order

walk_jitter.description: Structural assertions for jitter & seed (ADR 005 retains these from ADR 003 with the optimisation that jitter draws are SKIPPED when jitter==0 or op=='rest').

cases.name: jitter=0 ignores seed
cases.description: With jitter=0, no jitter draws are taken; output is seed-independent.

cases.name: jitter=1 (no rest cells) ignores cell op contents
cases.description: With jitter=1 and no rest cells, every authored op is replaced by a random pick from CELL_OPS={P,L,R,hold}. Result depends only on seed and pos, not on cell op identities.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(jitter=1, cells of {P,L,R,hold}) === walk(jitter=1, cells of all 'hold')

cases.name: rest is excluded from jitter substitution
cases.description: A cell with op='rest' never substitutes (jitter draws skipped). Walk with all-rest cells matches the no-rest no-op baseline regardless of jitter or seed.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(jitter=any, seed=any, cells of all rest) === startChord

cases.name: fixed seed reproduces
cases.description: Calling walk(state, pos) twice must yield identical triads.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(state, pos) === walk(state, pos) for arbitrary state, pos

cases.name: any-pos restart consistency
cases.description: walk(state, pos=N) must equal the result of advancing the walker step-by-step from pos=0 to N. Cross-target conformance depends on this — Live can resume playback at any position.
cases.structural_assertion: walk(state, N) === stepwise advance from 0 to N
